---
title: "Research"
subtitle: "Research lines, ongoing projects, and open-source software"
---

::: {.snuc-org-hero .compact}
<div class="snuc-org-hero-content">
<div class="snuc-eyebrow">What We Do</div>
<h1>Research</h1>
<p class="lead">Research lines, funded projects, and open-source software advancing the theory and methods of social network science.</p>
</div>
:::

## Research Areas

::: {.snuc-cards .areas}
<div class="snuc-card">**Social Network Analysis / Network Science**</div>
<div class="snuc-card">**Statistical Network Models**<br>ERGMs, SAOMs, Relational Event Models, Stochastic Block Models, etc.</div>
<div class="snuc-card">**Mathematical Sociology**</div>
<div class="snuc-card">**Computational Social Science**</div>
<div class="snuc-card">**Science of Science**</div>
<div class="snuc-card">**Governance Networks**</div>
<div class="snuc-card">**Organizational Networks**</div>
<div class="snuc-card">**Educational Networks**</div>
<div class="snuc-card">**Innovation and Scientific Collaboration Networks**</div>
<div class="snuc-card">**Network Data Science and Graph Machine Learning**</div>
<div class="snuc-card">**Methodological and Software Development for Network Analysis**</div>
:::

## Projects

::: {.snuc-projects}
<div class="snuc-project">
<div class="proj-meta"><span class="grant">ANID/FONDECYT Nº3250037 · 2025–2027</span><h3>Group Dynamics</h3></div>
<div class="proj-body">
<p class="desc">Understanding how social networks and individual characteristics co-evolve within group contexts.</p>
<p class="investigators">Principal Investigator: Alejandro Espinosa-Rada.</p>
</div>
</div>

<div class="snuc-project">
<div class="proj-meta"><span class="grant">ANID/FONDECYT Nº1261889 · 2026–2028</span><h3>New Approaches to the Study of Social Mobility</h3></div>
<div class="proj-body">
<p class="desc">Expanding theory, data, and methods for the study of social mobility.</p>
<p class="investigators">Principal Investigator: Mauricio Bucca. Co-Principal Investigators: Xi Song, Alejandro Espinosa-Rada.</p>
</div>
</div>

<div class="snuc-project">
<div class="proj-meta"><span class="grant">ANID/FONDECYT Nº1261268 · 2026–2028</span><h3>Metropolitan Governance</h3></div>
<div class="proj-body">
<p class="desc">The institutional complexity of metropolitan governance networks in Chile and Colombia.</p>
<p class="investigators">Principal Investigator: Karina Arias Yurisch. Co-Principal Investigators: Karina Retamal, Alejandro Espinosa-Rada, Cristian Leyton.</p>
</div>
</div>

<div class="snuc-project">
<div class="proj-meta"><span class="grant">Concurso Avanza UC 2025 · 2026–2027</span><h3>Governing Complexity</h3></div>
<div class="proj-body">
<p class="desc">Applying multilevel network analysis to the institutional framework of metropolitan environmental governance — part of the group's broader SNA-SES (Social Networks and Socio-ecological Sustainability) research line, alongside the UC–UNSW Seed Fund (2025–2026, with Pavel Krivitsky) and prior ANID/FONDECYT Nº1220560 (2022–2025).</p>
<p class="investigators">Responsible Investigator: Karina Arias. Principal Investigator: Alejandro Espinosa-Rada.</p>
</div>
</div>
:::

## Software

::: {.snuc-card}
### netmem
An R package for handling and transforming network data across formats commonly used in social network analysis, developed and maintained within the group.

- [CRAN page](https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/netmem/index.html)
- Install: `install.packages("netmem")`
:::

<!-- TODO: add further software tools/packages as they are released. -->
